第 18 章：数据转换器电路总览

原始标题：Basic Linear Design - Chapter 6: Data Converter Support Circuits · 来源：https://www.analog.com/media/en/training-seminars/design-handbooks/Basic-Linear-Design/Chapter6.pdf

说明：这是面向学习的中文详注 HTML，不是 ADI 官方中文译本。页面保留原文句子，并在每句下方给出中文译读和术语注释；涉及公式、参数和器件选择时，请回到 ADI 原文与对应数据手册复核。

本章中文详解重写讲义版

第 18 章：数据转换器电路总览

中文学习笔记，依据 ADI 原章节 重述并补充工程注释。不是逐句翻译，也不替代 ADI 原文与器件数据手册。

本章定位

ADI 原目录的第 18 章链接到《Basic Linear Design》第 6 章，共 216 页，覆盖 DAC、ADC、Σ-Δ、静态/动态误差、时序、数据手册阅读和选型。本章是一份导航式中文详解。

学习目标

  • 能用物理直觉说明主要电路或器件的工作过程。
  • 能写出本章核心近似式，并说明近似成立的条件。
  • 能识别输入范围、输出余量、功耗、噪声或稳定性中的主要限制。
  • 能阅读原理图并完成数量级检查。

DAC 架构

电阻串 DAC 单调性好但元件数指数增长；R-2R 梯形网络只需两种阻值；电流舵 DAC 适合高速；Σ-Δ DAC 用过采样和噪声整形换取高分辨率。分段结构常在面积、毛刺和线性度之间折中。

ADC 架构

Flash 速度极高但比较器数量大；SAR 逐次二分，功耗与速度平衡好；流水线用多级残差处理获得高速但有延迟；积分型擅长抑制工频；Σ-Δ 适合高分辨率、较窄带宽。

采样、混叠与驱动

采样把连续时间频谱周期复制，超过奈奎斯特带宽的能量会折叠进基带。抗混叠滤波负责限制带外能量，驱动器必须在采样瞬间给内部电容充电并在规定时间内建立。

静态误差

失调和增益误差可校准；DNL 描述相邻码宽偏差并决定单调性/丢码风险；INL 描述整体传输曲线偏离理想直线，影响低频精度和失真。

动态指标

SNR 只计噪声，SINAD 同时计噪声与失真，SFDR 看基波到最大杂散的距离，ENOB 由 SINAD 折算。孔径抖动在高输入频率下会限制可达 SNR。

阅读数据手册与选型

先定义信号带宽、幅度、允许误差、延迟、接口和功耗，再判断架构。数据手册典型曲线不能替代保证规格；还要核对参考源、输入网络、时钟、数字接口和评估板条件。

核心公式与使用条件

  • 量化步长：$LSB=FS/2^N$
  • 理想量化 SNR：$6.02N+1.76$ dB
  • $ENOB=(SINAD-1.76)/6.02$
  • 抖动限制：$SNR_j=-20\log_{10}(2\pi f_{in}t_j)$

公式默认采用理想或一阶模型。用于实际设计前，应补入器件容差、温度范围、频率依赖和数据手册保证值。

常见误区

  • 只按位数选转换器。
  • 混用 SNR、SINAD 与 SFDR。
  • 忽略参考源和驱动器的建立时间。

设计检查清单

  1. 先标出所有电压极性、电流方向与直流工作点。
  2. 验证器件处于假设的工作区，并检查输入/输出合规范围。
  3. 对最大、最小电源与温度角点重复计算。
  4. 计算功耗、噪声或带宽时写清单位和积分带宽。
  5. 用仿真或实验验证瞬态、稳定性及最坏容差。

复习题

  1. 用自己的话说明“DAC 架构”解决的核心问题。
  2. 针对“阅读数据手册与选型”，列出一个计算中必须检查的边界条件。
  3. 选取本章一幅图，沿信号或能量流向逐节点解释其工作过程。

来源与署名

  • 来源：ADI 原章节。
  • 原始教材及图片：Analog Devices, Inc.，用于非商业教育学习；保留 ADI 署名。
  • 本文中文讲解为重新组织的学习笔记；如与原文或最新数据手册不一致，以原始资料和器件数据手册为准。

原文逐句译读与注释按原站顺序

PDF 页面图与逐句译读原 PDF 已逐页渲染

这一章在 ADI 原站中以 PDF 形式提供。下面每个页面先给出 PDF 页面图，再给出抽取文本的中文译读和术语注释。由于 PDF 抽取文本可能存在换行或公式识别误差，设计计算请始终以页面图和原 PDF 为准。

PDF 第 1 页

展开本页文本译读与注释
原句Converters CHAPTER 6: CONVERTERS INTRODUCTION 6.1 SECTION 6.1: DIGITAL-TO-ANALOG CONVERTER ARCHITECTURES 6.3 DIGITAL-TO ANALOG CONVERTERS (DACs OR D/As) INTRODUCTION 6.3 KELVIN DIVIDER (STRING DACs) 6.4 SEGMENTED DACs 6.5 DIGITAL POTS 6.7 THERMOMETER (FULLY DECODED) DACs 6.9 BINARY WEIGHTED CURRENT SOURCES 6.12 R-2R LADDER 6.14 MULTIPLING DACs 6.18 SEGMENTED DACs 6.20 SIGMA-DELTA DACs 6.22 I/V CONVERTERS 6.23 DIFFERENTIAL TO SINGLE-ENDED CONVERSION TECHNIQUES 6.24 SINGLE-ENDED CURRENT-TO-VOLTAGE CONVERSION 6.27 DIFFERENTIAL CURRENT-TO-VOLTAGE CONVERSION 6.27 DIGITAL INTERFACES 6.28 DATA CONVERTER LOGIC: TIMING AND OTHER ISSUES 6.33 INTERPOLATING DACs (INTERPOLATING TxDACs) 6.33 RECONSTRUCTION FILTERS 6.35 SIN(X)/(X) (SINC) 6.36 INTENTIONALLY NONLINEAR DACs 6.37 SECTION 6.2: ANALOG-TO-DIGITAL CONVERTER ARCHITECTURES 6.40 THE COMPARATOR: A 1-BIT ADC 6.44 SUCCESSIVE APPROXIMATION ADCs 6.45 FLASH CONVERTERS 6.50 SUBRANGING, ERROR CORRECTED, AND PIPELINED ADCs 6.52 SERIAL BIT-PER-STAGE BINARY AND GRAY CODED (FOLDING) ADCs 6.58 COUNTING AND INTEGRATING ADC ARCHITECTURES 6.64 CHARGE RUN-DOWN ADCs 6.65 RAMP RUN-UP ADCs 6.65 TRACKING ADCs 6.66 VOLTAGE-TO-FREQUENCY CONVERTERS (VFCs) 6.68 DUAL-SLOPE/MULTISLOPE ADCs 6.73 RESOLVER-TO-DIGITAL CONVERTERS (RDCs) AND SYNCHROS 6.76
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 比较器 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释比较器：判断两个输入大小并输出逻辑电平；不能简单把普通运放当高速比较器。。

PDF 第 2 页

展开本页文本译读与注释
原句BASIC LINEAR DESIGN SECTION 6.2: ANALOG-TO-DIGITAL CONVERTER ARCHITECTURES (cont.) REFERENCES 6.80 SECTION 6.3: SIGMA-DELTA (ΣΔ) CONVERTERS 6.85 HISTORICAL PERSPECTIVE 6.85 BASICS OF SIGMA-DELTA ADCs 6.90 IDLE TONE CONSIDERATIONS 6.96 HIGHER ORDER LOOP CONSIDERATIONS 6.98 MULTIBIT SIGMA-DELTA CONVERTERS 6.98 DIGITAL FILTER IMPLICATIONS 6.100 HIGH RESOLUTION MEASUREMENT SIGMA-DELTA ADCs 6.102 BAND-PASS SIGMA-DELTA CONVERTERS 6.107 SIGMA-DELTA DACs 6.108 SUMMARY 6.110 REFERENCES 6.111 SECTION 6.4: DEFINING THE SPECIFICATIONS 6.115 SECTION 6.5: DAC AND ADC STATIC TRANSFER FUNCTIONS AND DC ERRORS 6.117 SECTION 6.6: DATA CONVERTER AC ERRORS 6.129 NOISE IN PRACTICAL ADCs 6.131 EQUIVALENT INPUT REFERRED NOISE 6.131 NOISE-FREE (FLICKER-FREE) CODE RESOLUTION 6.132 DYNAMIC PERFORMANCE OF DATA CONVERTERS 6.133 INTEGRAL AND DIFFERENTIAL NONLINEARITY DISTORTION EFFECTS 6.133 HARMONIC DISTORTION, WORST HARMONIC, TOTAL HARMONIC DISTORTION (THD), TOTAL HARMONIC DISTORTION PLUS NOISE (THD + N) 6.135 SIGNAL-TO-NOISE-AND-DISTORTION RATIO (SINAD), SIGNAL-TO-NOISE RATIO (SNR) AND EFFECTIVE NUMBER OF BITS (ENOB) 6.136 ANALOG BANDWIDTH 6.137 SPURIOUS-FREE DYNAMIC RANGE (SFDR) 6.138 TWO TONE INTERMODULATION DISTORTION (IMD) 6.141 MULTITONE SPURIOUS-FREE DYNAMIC RANGE 6.142 SECOND- AND THIRD-ORDER INTERCEPT POINTS, 1 dB COMPRESSION POINT 6.143 WIDEBAND CDMA (W-CDMA) ADJACENT CHANNEL POWER RATIO (ACPR) AND ADJACENT CHANNEL LEAKAGE RATIO (ADLR) 6.145 NOISE POWER RATIO (NPR) 6.146 NOISE FACTOR (F) AND NOISE FIGURE (NF) 6.149
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 带宽、噪声 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释带宽：满足指定增益/误差条件的频率范围。；噪声：电路内部随机扰动；低噪声设计要分清电压噪声、电流噪声和电阻热噪声。。

原句CONVERTERS INTRODUCTION 6.1 CHAPTER 6: CONVERTERS Introduction There are two basic type of converters, digi tal-to-analog (DACs or D/As) and analog-todigital (ADCs or A/Ds).
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Their purpose is fa irly straightforward.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句In the case of DACs, they output an analog voltage that is a pr oportion of a reference voltage, the proportion based on the digital word applied.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句In the case of the ADC, a digital representation of the analog voltage that is applied to the ADC s input is outputted, the representation proportional to a reference voltage.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句In both cases the digital word is almost al ways based on a binarily weighted proportion.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句The digital input or output is arranged in wo rds of varying widths, referred to as bits, typically anywhere from 6 bits to 24 bits.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句In a binarily weighted system each bit is worth half of the bit to its left and twice the bit to its right.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句The greater the number of bits in the digital word, the finer the reso lution.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句These bits ar e typically arranged in groups of four, called bytes, for convenience.

原句For a better understandi ng of the relationship between th e digital domain and the analog domain please refer to the section on sampling theory.

原句As stated earlier, we shall look at the opera tion of converters primarily from a “black box” view.

原句We will concern ourselves less with the internal construction of the converter and more with its operation.

原句The resolution of a converter is the number of bits in its digital word.

原句The accuracy is the number of those bits that m eet the specifications.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句For instance, a DAC might have 16 bits of resolution, but might onl y be monotonic to 14 bits.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 上述概念，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句This means that the assured accuracy of the DAC will be no better than 14 bits.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Also, an audio ADC might have a digital word width of 16 bits, but the SNR may be only 70 dB.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 上述概念，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句This means that the accuracy will only be at the 12-bit level.

原句CONVERTERS DIGITAL-TO-ANALOG CONVERTER ARCHITECTURES 6.3 SECTION 6.1: DIGITAL-TO-ANALOG CONVERTER ARCHITECTURES Digital-to-Analog Converters (DACs or D/As) Introduction What we commonly refer to as a DAC today is typically quite a bit more.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 数模转换器 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释数模转换器：把数字码转换为模拟电压或电流，核心指标包括单调性、INL、DNL、建立时间和噪声。。
原句The DAC will typically have the converter itself and a collection of support circuitry built into the chip.

原句The first DACs were board level designs, built from discrete components, including vacuum tubes as the switching elements.

原句These early examples were actually su b-blocks of the DAC.

原句An example of this would be the AD550, which was a 4 bit binarily weighted current source.

原句This current source block would be mated to a separate part, such as the AD 850, which contained a resistor array and CMOS switches.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 CMOS 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释CMOS：互补 MOS 技术，静态功耗低，是现代数字/混合信号电路基础。。
原句Together these would form the basic DAC.

原句Then a second rank of latches was often added.

原句BASIC LINEAR DESIGN 6.4 On the back end, since the output of the DAC is often a current, an op amp is often added to perform the current-to-voltage (I/V) conversion.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 运放 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释运放：高增益差分放大器；分析时先确认是否处于负反馈线性区。。
原句On the front end a voltage reference is often added.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 电压基准 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释电压基准：提供稳定、低噪声、低漂移的参考电压，是 ADC/DAC 精度链路的一部分。。
原句Process limitations did not allow the integration of all these sub-blocks to occur at once.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句Initially, the processes used to make the va rious sub-blocks were not compatible.

原句Probably more important is that the circuit designer now doesn’t have to concern hi mself with the accuracy of se veral parts in a system.

原句Next we will look at the various DAC architectures.

原句When we refer to DACs here we are referring to the basic converter rather than the complete system.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句Kelvin Divider (String DAC) The simplest structure of all is the Kelvin divider or string DAC as shown in Figure 6.2.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句An N-bit version of this DAC simply consists of 2 N equal resistors in series and 2 N switches (usually CMOS), one between each node of the chain and the output.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 CMOS 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释CMOS：互补 MOS 技术，静态功耗低，是现代数字/混合信号电路基础。。
原句The output is taken from the appropriate tap by closing ju st one of the switches (t here is some slight digital complexity involved in decoding to 1 of 2N switches from N-bit data).
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句This architecture is simple, has a voltage out put and is inherently monotonic—even if a resistor is accidentally shor t-circuited, output n cannot exceed output n + 1.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句It is linear if all the resistors are equal, but may be made deliberately nonlinear if a nonlinear DAC is required.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 安全工作范围、精度或稳定性。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句The output is a voltage, but it has the disadvantage of ha ving a relatively large output impedance.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 阻抗，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。
补充注释阻抗：交流中电压与电流的复数比，包含电阻和电抗。。
原句This output impedance is also code dependant (the impedance changes with changes to the digital input).
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 阻抗 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释阻抗：交流中电压与电流的复数比，包含电阻和电抗。。
原句In many cases it will be beneficial to follow the output of the DAC with an op amp to buffer this output impedance and present a low impedance source to the following circuitry.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 运放、阻抗 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释运放：高增益差分放大器；分析时先确认是否处于负反馈线性区。；阻抗：交流中电压与电流的复数比，包含电阻和电抗。。
原句Since only two switches operate during a transition it is a low glitch architecture (the concept of glitch will be examined in a fo llowing section).
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Also, the switching glitch is not code-dependent, making it ideal for low di stortion applications.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Because the glitch is constant regardless of the code transition, th e frequency content of the glitch is at the DAC update rate and its harmonics—not at the harmonics of the DAC output signal frequency.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句The major drawback of the Kelvin DAC is the large num ber of resistors and switches required for high resolution.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 安全工作范围、精度或稳定性。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句There are 2 N resistors required, so a 10 bit DAC would require 1024 switches and resistors, a nd as a result it was not commonly used as a simple DAC architecture until the recent advent of very small IC feature sizes made it very practical for low and medium resolution (typically up to 10 bits) DACs.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 安全工作范围、精度或稳定性。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。

展开本页文本译读与注释
原句CONVERTERS DIGITAL-TO-ANALOG CONVERTER ARCHITECTURES 6.5 Figure 6.2: Simplest Voltage-Output Thermometer DAC: The Kelvin Divider As we mentioned in the section on sampling th eory, the output of a DAC for an all 1s code is 1 LSB below the reference, so a Kelvin divider DAC intended for use as a general-purpose DAC has a resistor between the reference terminal and the first switch as shown in Figure 6.2.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句Since there are buffers be tween the first and second stages, the second string DAC does not load the first, and the re sistors in the second string do not need to have the same value as the resistors in the first.

原句All the resistors in each string, however, do need to be equal to each other or the DAC will not be linear.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 上述概念，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。

原句The examples shown have 3-bit first and second stages but for the sa ke of generality, let us refer to the first (MSB) stage resolution as M-bits and the second (LSB) as K-bits for a total of N = M + K bits.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 上述概念，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。

原句The MSB DAC has a string of 2 M equal resistors and a string of 2 K equal resistors in the LSB DAC.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句As an example, if we make a 10-bit string DAC out of two 5-bit sections, each segment would have 2 5 or 32 resistors, for a total of 64, as opposed to the 1024 required for a standard Kelvin divider.

原句BASIC LINEAR DESIGN 6.6 Figure 6.3: Segmented Voltage-Output DACs Buffer amplifiers can have offset, of c ourse, and this can cause nonmonotonicity in a buffered segmented string DAC.

原句In the simpler configuration of a buffered Kelvin-Varley divider buffer (Figure 6.3A), buffer A is always “below” (at a lower potenti al than) buffer B, and the extra tap labeled “A” on the LSB string DAC is not necessary.

原句The data decoding is just two priority encoders.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句But if the decoding of the MSB string DAC is made more complex so that buffer A can only be connected to the taps labeled “A” in the MSB string DAC, and buffer B to the taps labeled “B,” then it is not possible for buffer offsets to cause nonmonotonicity.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 上述概念，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。

原句Of course, the LSB string DAC decoding must change direction each time one buffer “leapfrogs” the other, and taps A and B on the LSB string DAC are alternately not used—but this involves a fairly trivial increase in logic complexity and is justified by the increased performance.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 安全工作范围、精度或稳定性。

原句Rather than using a second string of resist ors, a binary R-2R DAC can be used to generate the three LSBs as shown in Figure 6.3B.
中文译读可译为“它可用于/常用于……”。本句强调 该结构 的用途，设计时还要检查适用条件和限制。

原句In most digital potentiometers the voltage on the input pins can not exceed the supplies (typically 3 V or 5 V) due to the CMOS switc hes used in their construction, but certain models are designed for ±15 V operation.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 CMOS，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。

原句Another design feature on many of the digi tal potentiometers is that on power up (sometimes from an internal timer, sometimes c ontrolled by an extern al pin) the wiper is shorted to one of the terminals.

展开本页文本译读与注释
原句BASIC LINEAR DESIGN 6.10 Figure 6.7: The Simplest Current-Output Thermometer (Fully Decoded) DAC A DAC where the currents are switched between two output lines—one of which is often grounded, but may, in the more general case, be used as the inverted output—is more suitable for high speed applications because switching a current between two outputs is far less disruptive, and so causes a far lower glitch than simply switching a current on and off.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 上述概念，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。

原句In addition, the outpu t impedance of the voltagemode binary DAC changes with the input code.

原句Figure 6.16: DAC-08 Block Diagram There are two ways in which the R-2R la dder network may be used as a DAC—known respectively as the voltage mode and the current mode (they are sometimes called “normal” mode and “inverted” mode, but as there is no consensus on whether the voltage mode or the current mode is the “normal” mode for a ladder network this nomenclature can be misleading, although in most cases th e current mode would be considered the “normal” mode).
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 上述概念，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Each mode has its advantages and disadvantages.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句In the current-mode R-2R ladder DAC shown in Figure 6.17, the gain of the DAC may be adjusted with a series resistor at the V REF terminal, since in the current mode, the end of the ladder, with its code-independent impedance, is used as the V REF terminal; and the ends of the arms are switched between ground a nd an output line which must be held at ground potential.
中文译读可译为“它可用于/常用于……”。本句强调 阻抗 的用途，设计时还要检查适用条件和限制。
补充注释阻抗：交流中电压与电流的复数比，包含电阻和电抗。。
原句The normal connection of a current-mode ladder network output is to an op amp’s inverting input (virtual ground), but stabilization of this op amp is complicated by the DAC output impedance variation with digital code.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 运放、阻抗，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。
补充注释运放：高增益差分放大器；分析时先确认是否处于负反馈线性区。；阻抗：交流中电压与电流的复数比，包含电阻和电抗。。

原句If switches capable of carrying current in either direction (such as CMOS devices) are used, the reference voltage may have either polarity, or may even be ac.
中文译读可译为“它可用于/常用于……”。本句强调 CMOS 的用途，设计时还要检查适用条件和限制。
补充注释CMOS：互补 MOS 技术，静态功耗低，是现代数字/混合信号电路基础。。
原句Such a structure is one of the most common types used as a multiplying DAC (MDAC) which will be discussed later in this section.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句Since the switches are always at, or very close to, ground potential, the maximum reference voltage may greatly exceed the logic voltage, provided the switches are makebefore-break—which they ar e in this type of DAC.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句It is not unknown for a CMOS MDAC to accept a ±30 V reference (or even a 60-V peak-to-peak ac reference) while working from a single 5 V supply.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 CMOS 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释CMOS：互补 MOS 技术，静态功耗低，是现代数字/混合信号电路基础。。
原句In the voltage mode R-2R ladder DAC show n in Figure 6.18, the “rungs” or arms of the ladder are switched between V REF and ground, and the output is taken from the end of the ladder.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句The output may be taken as a voltage, but the output impedance is independent of code, so it may equally well be taken as a current into a virtual ground.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 阻抗，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。
补充注释阻抗：交流中电压与电流的复数比，包含电阻和电抗。。
原句The voltage output is an advantage of this mo de, as is the constant output impedance, which eases the stabilization of any amplifie r connected to the output node.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 阻抗 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释阻抗：交流中电压与电流的复数比，包含电阻和电抗。。
原句Additionally, the switches switch the arms of the ladder between a low impedance V REF connection and ground, which is also, of course, low impedance, so capacitive glitch currents tend not to flow in the load.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 阻抗 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释阻抗：交流中电压与电流的复数比，包含电阻和电抗。。
原句On the other hand, the switc hes must operate over a wide voltage range (V REF to ground), which is difficult from a de sign and manufacturing viewpoint, and the reference input impedance varies widely with code, so that the refe rence input must be driven from a very low impedance.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 阻抗。
补充注释阻抗：交流中电压与电流的复数比，包含电阻和电抗。。
原句In addi tion, the gain of the DAC cannot be adjusted by means of a resistor in series with the VREF terminal.

原句BASIC LINEAR DESIGN 6.20 The frequency response of the MDAC is lim ited by the parasitic ca pacitance across the switches in the off condition.

原句As the frequenc y goes up the impedance of the capacitors goes down, effectively bypassing the switch.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 阻抗 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释阻抗：交流中电压与电流的复数比，包含电阻和电抗。。
原句Th is reduces the off isolation at higher frequencies.

原句BASIC LINEAR DESIGN 6.22 Figure 6.23: AD9775 TxDAC 14-Bit CMOS DAC Core Decoding must be done before the new data is applied to the DAC so that all the data is ready and can be applied simultaneously to all the switches in the DAC.

中文译读可译为“它可用于/常用于……”。本句强调 运放 的用途，设计时还要检查适用条件和限制。
补充注释运放：高增益差分放大器；分析时先确认是否处于负反馈线性区。。
原句The output current is dumped into a resistor instead of into an op amp directly since the fast edges may exceed the slew rate of the amplifier and cause distortion.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 运放、压摆率 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释运放：高增益差分放大器；分析时先确认是否处于负反馈线性区。；压摆率：输出电压变化速度上限；大信号高频时常比小信号带宽更先限制波形。。
原句Many DACs supply full-scal e currents of 20 mA or more, thereby allowing reasonable voltages to be developed ac ross fairly low value load resistors.

原句If the DAC is fabricated on a bipolar or BiCMOS process, it is likely that the output will sink current, and that the output impedance will be less than 500 Ω (due to the internal R/2R resistive ladder network).
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 阻抗、CMOS 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释阻抗：交流中电压与电流的复数比，包含电阻和电抗。；CMOS：互补 MOS 技术，静态功耗低，是现代数字/混合信号电路基础。。
原句On the other hand, a CMOS DAC is more likely to source output current and have a high output impedance, typically greater than 100 kΩ.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 阻抗、CMOS 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。

原句Figure 6.25: Model of High Speed DAC Output Another considera tion is the output compliance voltage— the maximum voltage swing allowed at the output in order for the DAC to maintain its linearity.

原句In fact, most high reso lution DACs of this type are factory trimmed using an I/V converter.

原句It should be recalled, however, that usi ng the single-ended output of the DAC in this manner will cause degradation in the CM rejection and increased second-order distortion products, compared to a differential operating mode.

原句A DAC without a latch, or with a single latch, must be loaded in parallel with all bits at once, since otherwise its output during loading may be totally different from what it was or what it is to become.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 安全工作范围、精度或稳定性。

原句A double-buffered DAC, on the other hand, may be loaded with parallel data, or with serial data, or with 4-bit or 8-bit words, or wh atever, and the output will be IOUT IOUT 0 TO 20mA 0 TO +0.5V 20 TO 0mA +0.5 TO 0V CMOS DAC + – AD813X VOCM 2.49kΩ 2.49kΩ 5V p-p DIFFERENTIAL OUTPUT 25Ω 25Ω 499Ω 499Ω IOUT IOUT 0 TO 20mA 0 TO +0.5V 20 TO 0mA +0.5 TO 0V CMOS DAC + – AD813X VOCM 2.49kΩ 2.49kΩ 5V p-p DIFFERENTIAL OUTPUT 25Ω 25Ω 499Ω 499Ω

原句CONVERTERS DIGITAL-TO-ANALOG CONVERTER ARCHITECTURES 6.29 unaffected until the new data is completely loaded and the DAC receives its update instruction.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句Figure 6.31: Double-Buffered DAC Permits Complex Input Structures and Simultaneous Update The other convenience of the double-buffered structure is that many DACs may be updated simultaneously: data is loaded into the first rank of each DAC in turn, and when all is ready, the output buffers of all the DACs are updated at once.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句There are many DAC applications where the output of a number of DACs must change simultaneously, and the double-buffered structure allows this to be done very easily.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 安全工作范围、精度或稳定性。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Most early monolithic high resolution DACs ha d parallel or byte-wide data ports and tended to be connected to pa rallel data buses and addre ss decoders and addressed by microprocessors as if they were very sm all write-only memories.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句(Some parallel DACs are not write-only, but can have their contents read as well—this is convenient for some applications, but is not very common.) A DAC connected to a data bus is vulnerable to capacitive coupling of logic noise from the bus to the analog output Serial interfaces are less vulnerable to such noise (since fewer noi sy pins are involved), use fewer pins, and therefore take less board space, and are freque ntly more convenient for use with modern microprocessors, most of which have serial da ta ports.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 噪声，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。
补充注释噪声：电路内部随机扰动；低噪声设计要分清电压噪声、电流噪声和电阻热噪声。。
原句Some, but not all, of such serial DACs have data outputs as well as data inputs so that several DACs may be connected in series and data clocked to them all from a single serial port.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 上述概念，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句This arrangement is often referred to as "daisy-chaining." Of course, serial DACs cannot be used wher e high update rates are involved, since the clock rate of the serial data would be too high.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句Some very high speed DACs actually have two parallel data ports and use them alternat ely in a multiplexed fashion (sometimes this is called a “ping-pong” input) to reduce the data rate on each port as shown in Figure 6.32.
中文译读可译为“这种对象/现象称为……”。本句是在给 一个术语 下定义；后文遇到该词时要回到这个定义。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句The alternate loading (ping-pong) DAC in the diagram loads from port A and port B alternately on the rising and fa lling edges of the clock, which must have a mark-space ratio close to 50:50.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 安全工作范围、精度或稳定性。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句The internal clock multiplier ensures that the DAC itself is updated with data A and data B alternatel y at exactly 50:50 time ratio, even if the external clock is not so precise.

原句BASIC LINEAR DESIGN 6.30 Figure 6.32: Alternate Loading (Ping-Pong) High Speed DAC Historically integrated circuit logic circuitry (with the exception of emitter coupled logic or ECL) operated from 5 V supplies and had compatible logic levels—with a few exceptions 5 V logic would interface with othe r 5 V logic.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句Today, with the advent of low voltage logic operating with supplies of 3.3 V, 2.7 V, or even less, it is important to ensure that logic interfaces are compatible.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句There are several issues which must be considered—absolute maximum ratings, wors t case logic levels, and timing.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 安全工作范围、精度或稳定性。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句The logic inputs of integrated circuits generally have absolute maximum ratings, as do most other inputs, of 300 mV outside the power supply.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 电源 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释电源：为电路提供能量；要同时考虑电压、电流、纹波、瞬态和保护。。
原句Note that these are instantaneous ratings.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句If an IC has such a rating and is currently operating from a +5 V supply then the logic inputs may be between –0.3 V and +5.3 V—but if the supply is not present then that input must be between +0.3 V and –0.3 V, not the –0.3 V to +5.3 V which are the limits once the power is applied—ICs cannot predict the future.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 安全工作范围、精度或稳定性。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句The reason for the rating of 0.3 V is to ensure th at no parasitic diode inside the IC is ever turned on by a voltage outside the IC’s absolute maximum ra ting.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 二极管 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释二极管：单向导电非线性器件；实际行为与电流、温度和反向恢复有关。。
原句It is quite common to protect an input from such over-voltage with a Schottky diode clamp.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 二极管 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释二极管：单向导电非线性器件；实际行为与电流、温度和反向恢复有关。。
原句At low temperatures the clamp voltage of a Schottky diode may be a little more than 0.3 V, and so the IC may see voltages just outside it s absolute maximum rating.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 二极管 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释二极管：单向导电非线性器件；实际行为与电流、温度和反向恢复有关。。
原句Although, strictly speaking, this subjects the IC to stresses outside it s absolute maximum ratings and so is forbidden, this is an acceptable exception to the general rule provided the Schottky diode is at a similar temperature to the IC that it is protecting (say within ±10°C).
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 二极管，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。
补充注释二极管：单向导电非线性器件；实际行为与电流、温度和反向恢复有关。。
原句Some low voltage devices, however, have i nputs with absolute maximum ratings which are substantially greater than their supply voltage .
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 上述概念，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句This allows such circuits to be driven by higher voltage logic without additional interface or clamp circuitry.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句But it is important to read the data sheets and ensure that bot h logic levels and absolute maximum voltages are compatible for all combinations of high and low supplies.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 上述概念，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句This is the general rule when interfacing different low-voltage logic circuitry—it is always necessary to check both that at the lowest value of its power supply the logic 1 DATA MULTIPLEXER DAC LATCH DAC N N LATCH A LATCH B ×2 CLOCK MULTIPLIER N N N N DATA PORT A DATA PORT B CLOCK (50% DUTY CYCLE) OUTPUT OUTPUT
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 电源 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释电源：为电路提供能量；要同时考虑电压、电流、纹波、瞬态和保护。。

原句At some point a batch of devices near the limit on low output swing will be required to drive some devices needing slightly more drive than usual—and will be unable to do so.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 安全工作范围、精度或稳定性。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句One of the latest developments in high sp eed logic interface is LVDS.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Low voltage differential signaling (LVDS) presents a solu tion to the high speed converter interface problem by mitigating the effects of CMOS single-ended interfaces and accommodating higher data rates.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 CMOS 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释CMOS：互补 MOS 技术，静态功耗低，是现代数字/混合信号电路基础。。
原句The LVDS standard speci fies a p-p voltage swing of 350 mV around a common-mode voltage of 1.2 V, which facilita tes transmission of high-speed differential digital signals with balanced current, th ereby reducing the slew rate requirement.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 压摆率 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释压摆率：输出电压变化速度上限；大信号高频时常比小信号带宽更先限制波形。。
原句Reducing the slew rate elimin ates the gradients that resu lt in noise from ground bounce that are present in conventional CMOS drivers.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 压摆率、噪声、CMOS 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释压摆率：输出电压变化速度上限；大信号高频时常比小信号带宽更先限制波形。；噪声：电路内部随机扰动；低噪声设计要分清电压噪声、电流噪声和电阻热噪声。；CMOS：互补 MOS 技术，静态功耗低，是现代数字/混合信号电路基础。。
原句Ground-bounce noise can couple back into sensitive analog circuits and degrade the converter's dynamic range.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 噪声 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释噪声：电路内部随机扰动；低噪声设计要分清电压噪声、电流噪声和电阻热噪声。。
原句Parallel LVDS interfaces enable much highe r data rates and optimum dyn amic performance, in highspeed data converters.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句LVDS also offers some benefit in reduced EMI.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句Trace length, skew, and discontinuities will reduce this benefit and should be avoided.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句LVDS also offers simpler timing constraint s compared to a demuxed CMOS solution at similar data rates.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 CMOS 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释CMOS：互补 MOS 技术，静态功耗低，是现代数字/混合信号电路基础。。
原句A demuxed databus re quires a synchronization signal that is not required in LVDS.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 安全工作范围、精度或稳定性。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句In demuxed CMOS buses, a clock equal to one-h alf the ADC sample rate is needed, adding cost a nd complexity, that is not requi red in LVDS.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 CMOS 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释CMOS：互补 MOS 技术，静态功耗低，是现代数字/混合信号电路基础。。
原句In general, the LVDS is more forgiving and can lead to a simpler, cleaner design.

原句Layout Guidelines Keep T W, TS, and D constant over the trace length Keep T S ~ < 2 TW Avoid use of vias where possible Keep D > 2 T S Avoid 90° bends if possible Design T W and TG for ~50 Ω Figure 6.34: PCB Trace Spacing Power supply decoupling is very important w ith these fast (<0.5 ns) edge rates.

原句CONVERTERS DIGITAL-TO-ANALOG CONVERTER ARCHITECTURES 6.33 Data Converter Logic: Timing and other Issues It is not the purpose of this brief section to discuss logic architect ures, so we shall not define the many different data converter logic interface operati ons and their timing specifications except to note that data conve rter logic interfaces may be more complex than you expect—do not expect that because there is a pin with the same name on memory and interface chips it will behave in exactly the same way in a data converter.

原句CONVERTERS DIGITAL-TO-ANALOG CONVERTER ARCHITECTURES 6.37 Intentionally Nonlinear DACs Thus far, we have emphasized the impor tance of maintaining good differential and integral linearity.

原句One of the earliest uses of nonlinear data converters was in the digi tization of voice-band signals for pulse code modulation (PCM) systems.

原句Major contributio ns were made at Bell Labs during the development of the T1 carrier system.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句The motive for the nonlinear ADCs and DACs was to reduce the total number of bi ts (and therefore the serial transmission rate) required to digitize voice channels.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 安全工作范围、精度或稳定性。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Straight linear encodi ng of a voice channel required 11-bits or 12-bits at an 8 kSPS per channel sampling rate.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 安全工作范围、精度或稳定性。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句In the 1960s Bell Labs determined that 7-bit nonlinear encoding was sufficient, and later in the 1970s went to 8-bit nonlinear encoding for better performance.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句The nonlinear transfer function allocates more quantization levels out of the total range for small signals and fewer for large amplitude signals.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 小信号 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释小信号：在直流工作点附近线性化后的增量模型，只适合小扰动。。
原句In effect, this reduces the quantization noise associated with small si gnals (where it is most noticeable) and increases the quantization noise for larger signals (where it is less noticeable).
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 噪声 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释噪声：电路内部随机扰动；低噪声设计要分清电压噪声、电流噪声和电阻热噪声。。
原句The term companding is generally used to describe this form of encoding.
中文译读可译为“它可用于/常用于……”。本句强调 该结构 的用途，设计时还要检查适用条件和限制。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句The logarithmic transfer function chosen is re ferred to as the “Bell µ-255” standard, or simply “µ-law.” A similar standard developed in Europe is referred to as “A-law.” The Bell µ-law allows a dynamic range of about 40 00:1 using 8 bits, whereas an 8-bit linear data converter provides a range of only 256:1.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句The first generation channel bank (D1) us ed temperature contro lled resistor-diode networks for “compressors” ahead of a 7-bit linear ADC in the transmitter to generate the logarithmic transfer functi on.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 二极管 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释二极管：单向导电非线性器件；实际行为与电流、温度和反向恢复有关。。
原句Corresponding resistor-diode “expandors” having an inverse transfer function followed the 7-b it linear DAC in the receiver.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 二极管 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释二极管：单向导电非线性器件；实际行为与电流、温度和反向恢复有关。。
原句The next generation D2 channel banks used nonlinear ADCs and DACs to accomplish the compression/expansion functions in a much mo re reliable and cost-effective manner and eliminated the need for the temperature-controlled diode networks.

原句The basic DAC in the LOGDAC is a linear 17-bit voltage-m ode R-2R DAC preceded by an 8-bit input decoder (A functional diagram of the LOGDAC is shown in Figure 6.40).

原句In addition to providing the logarithmic transfer function, the LOGDAC al so acts as a full four-quadrant multiplying DAC.

原句With the introduction of high resolution lin ear ADCs and DACs, the method used in the LOGDAC is widely used today to implement various nonlinear transfer functions such as the µ-law and A-law companding functions required for telecommunications and other applications.

原句A big advantage of this method is that the transfer curve does not have to be approximated with straight line segments as in the earlier method, thereby providing more accuracy.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 安全工作范围、精度或稳定性。

原句Twenty years ago it was common for converter references to have accuracies as poor as ±5% since these references were trimmed for low temperature coefficient rather than absolute accuracy, and the inaccuracy of the reference was compensated in the gain trim of the ADC itself.

原句The reference input terminal of an ADC may be buffered as shown in Figure 6.43, in which case it has input impedance (usually high) and bias current (usually low) specifications, or it may connect directly to the ADC.

补充注释阻抗：交流中电压与电流的复数比，包含电阻和电抗。。
原句In either case, the transient currents developed on the reference input due to the internal conversion process need good decoupling with external low inductance cap acitors.

原句Regardless of the ADC, it is extremely important to read the data sheet and determine exactly what the external clock requirements are, because they can vary widely from one ADC to another.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句At some point after the assertion of the sampling clock, the output data is valid.

原句This data may be in parallel or serial format depending upon the ADC.

原句Early successive approximation ADCs such as the AD574 si mply provided a STATUS output (STS) which went high during the conversion, and retu rned to the low state when the output data was valid.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句In other ADCs, this line is variously called busy, end-of-conversion (EOC), data ready, etc.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句Regardless of the ADC, ther e must be some method of knowing when the output data is valid—and again, the data sheet is where this information can always be found.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 安全工作范围、精度或稳定性。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Another detail which can cau se trouble is the differen ce between EOC and DRDY (data ready).
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句EOC indicates that conve rsion has finished, DRDY that data is available at the output.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句In some ADCs, EOC functions as DRDY—in others, data is not valid until several tens of nanoseconds after the EOC has become valid, and if EOC is used as a data strobe, the results will be unreliable.
中文译读可译为“它可用于/常用于……”。本句强调 该结构 的用途，设计时还要检查适用条件和限制。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句There are one or two other pr actical points which are worth remembering about the logic of ADCs.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句On power-up, many ADCs do not have logic reset circuitry and may enter an anomalous logical state.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Severa l conversions may be necessar y to restore their logic to proper operation so: (a) the first few conversio ns after power-up should never be trusted, and (b) control outputs (EOC, data ready, etc. ) may behave in unexpected ways at this time (and not necessarily in the same way at each power-up), and (c) care should be taken to ensure that such anomalous behavior cannot cause system latch-up.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句For example, EOC (end-of-conversion) should not be used to in itiate conversion if there is any possibility that EOC will not occur until th e first conversion has taken place, as otherwise initiation will never occur.
中文译读可译为“它可用于/常用于……”。本句强调 该结构 的用途，设计时还要检查适用条件和限制。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Some low-power ADCs now have power-sav ing modes of operation variously called standby, power-down, sleep, etc.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句When an ADC comes out of one of these low-power modes, there is a certain recovery time re quired before the ADC can operate at its full specified performance.

原句The data sheet should therefore be carefully studied when using these modes of operation.

原句As a final example, some ADCs use CS (Chip Select) edges to reset internal logic, and it may not be possible to perform another conve rsion without asserti ng or reasserting CS (or it may not be possible to read the same data twice, or both).

原句BASIC LINEAR DESIGN 6.44 The Comparator: A 1-Bit ADC A comparator is a 1-bit ADC (see Figure 6.44).
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 比较器 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释比较器：判断两个输入大小并输出逻辑电平；不能简单把普通运放当高速比较器。。
原句If the input is above a threshold, the output has one logic value, below it has an other.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句There is no ADC architecture which does not use at least one comparator of some sort.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 比较器 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释比较器：判断两个输入大小并输出逻辑电平；不能简单把普通运放当高速比较器。。
原句So while a 1 bit ADC is of very limited usefulness it is a building block for other architectures.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Figure 6.44: The Comparator: A 1-Bit ADC Comparators used as building blocks in ADCs need good resolution which implies high gain.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 比较器 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释比较器：判断两个输入大小并输出逻辑电平；不能简单把普通运放当高速比较器。。
原句This can lead to uncont rolled oscillation when the differential input approaches zero.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句In order to prevent this, hysteresis is often added to comparators using a small amount of positive feedback.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 正反馈、比较器 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释正反馈：反馈信号强化输入误差，常用于比较器迟滞或振荡器；线性放大中要谨慎。；比较器：判断两个输入大小并输出逻辑电平；不能简单把普通运放当高速比较器。。
原句Figure 6.44 shows the effects of hysteresis on the overall transfer function.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Many comparators have a millivolt or two of hysteresis to encourage “snap” action and to prevent local feedback from causing instability in the transition region.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 比较器、稳定性 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释比较器：判断两个输入大小并输出逻辑电平；不能简单把普通运放当高速比较器。；稳定性：反馈电路不会自激振荡并具有足够瞬态裕量。。
原句Note that the resolution of the comparat or can be no less th an the hysteresis, so large values of hysteresis are generally not useful. + – DIFFERENTIAL ANALOG INPUT LOGIC OUTPUT LATCH ENABLE DIFFERENTIAL ANALOG INPUT COMPARATOR OUTPUT "0" "1" 0 VHYSTERESIS
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 比较器 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释比较器：判断两个输入大小并输出逻辑电平；不能简单把普通运放当高速比较器。。

原句CONVERTERS ANALOG-TO-DIGITAL CONVERTER ARCHITECTURES 6.45 Successive Approximation ADCs The successive approximation ADC has been the mainstay of data acquisition for many years.

原句Recent design improvements have extended the sampling frequency of these ADCs into the megahertz region.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句The basic successive approximation ADC is shown in Figure 6.45.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句It performs conversions on command.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句On the asserti on of the CONVERT START command, the sample-and-hold (SHA) is placed in the hold mode, and all the bits of the successive approximation register (SAR) are reset to “0 ” except the MSB which is set to “1.” The SAR output drives the internal DAC.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句If the DAC output is greater than the analog input, this bit in the SAR is reset, otherwise it is left set.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句The next most significant bit is then set to “1.” If the DAC output is greater than th e analog input, this bit in the SAR is reset, otherwise it is left set.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句The process is repe ated with each bit in turn.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句When all the bits have been set, tested, and reset or not as a ppropriate, the contents of the SAR correspond to the value of the analog input, and the c onversion is complete.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句These bit “tests” can form the basis of a serial output version SAR-based ADC.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句The end of conversion is generally indicated by an end-of-convert (EOC), data-ready (DRDY), or a busy signal (actually, not-BUSY indicates end of conversion).

原句The polarities and name of this signal may be different for different SAR ADCs, but the fundamental concept is the same.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 上述概念，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。

原句At the be ginning of the conversi on interval, the signal goes high (or low) and remains in that state until the conversion is completed, at which time it goes low (or high).
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句The trailing edge is generally an indi cation of valid output SHA CONTROL LOGIC: SUCCESSIVE APPROXIMATION REGISTER (SAR)DAC TIMING CONVERT START EOC, DRDY, OR BUSY OUTPUT ANALOG INPUT COMPARATOR

原句It would seem on superficial examination that a 16-bit converter would have twice the conversion time of an 8-bit one, but this is not the case.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 上述概念，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。

原句In an 8-bit converter, the DAC must settle to 8-bit accuracy before the bit decision is made, whereas in a 16-bit converter, it must settle to 16-bit accuracy, which takes a lot longer.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 安全工作范围、精度或稳定性。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句In practice, 8-bit successive approximation ADCs can convert in a few hundred nanoseconds, while 16-bit ones will generally take several microseconds.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句While there are some variations, the fundame ntal timing of most SAR ADCs is similar and relatively straightforward.

原句This signal is a usually a negative-goi ng pulse whose positive-going edge actually initiates the conversion.

原句The internal sample-and-hold (SHA) amplifier is placed in the hold mode on this edge, and th e various bits are determined using the SAR algorithm.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句The negative-going edge of the CONVST pulse causes a signal typically called EOC (End Of Conversion) or BUSY to go high.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句Wh en the conversion is complete, the BUSY line goes low (or EOC goes high), indicating the completion of the conversion process.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句In most cases the trailing edge of the BUSY line can be used as an indication that the output data is valid and can be used to strobe the output data into an external register.

原句The frequency of the exte rnal clock, if required, generally falls in the range of 1 MHz to 30 MHz depending on the conversion time and resolution of the ADC.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 安全工作范围、精度或稳定性。

原句Other SAR ADCs have an internal os cillator which is used to perform the conversions and only require the CONVE RT START command.
中文译读可译为“它可用于/常用于……”。本句强调 该结构 的用途，设计时还要检查适用条件和限制。

原句Because of their architecture, SAR ADCs generally allow singl e-shot conversion at any repetition rate from dc to the converter's maximum conversion rate.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句Notice that the overall accuracy and linearity of the SAR ADC is determined primarily by the internal DAC.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句Until recently, most precision SAR ADCs used laser-trimmed thin-film DACs to achieve the desired accuracy and linearity.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句The thin-fil m resistor trimming process adds cost, and the thin-film resistor values may be affected when subjected to the mechanical stresses of packaging.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句For these reasons, switched capacitor (or ch arge-redistribution) DACs have become popular in newer SAR ADCs.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Th e advantage of the switched capacitor DAC is that the accuracy and linearity is primarily dete rmined by photolithography, which in turn controls the capacitor plate area and the capacitance as well as matching.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 电容 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释电容：储存电荷的能力；阻抗随频率升高而降低。。
原句In addition, small capacitors can be placed in parallel with the main capacitors which can be switched in and out under control of autocalibration routines to achieve high accuracy and linearity without the need for thin-film laser trimming.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Temperature tracking between the switched capacitors can be better than 1 ppm/ºC, thereby offering a high degree of temperature stability.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 稳定性 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释稳定性：反馈电路不会自激振荡并具有足够瞬态裕量。。
原句Figure 6.47: 3-Bit Switched Capacitor DAC _ + _ + C/ 4C/ 2CC / 4 AIN VREF SIN SC S1 S2 S3 S4 BIT1 (MSB) BIT2 BIT3 (LSB) SWITCHES SHOWN IN TRACK (SAMPLE) MODE AA CTOTAL = 2C

展开本页文本译读与注释
原句BASIC LINEAR DESIGN 6.50 Flash Converters Flash ADCs (sometimes called parallel ADCs) are the fastest t ype of ADC and use large numbers of comparators.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 比较器 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释比较器：判断两个输入大小并输出逻辑电平；不能简单把普通运放当高速比较器。。
原句An N- bit flash ADC consists of 2 N resistors and 2 N–1 comparators arranged as in Figure 6.50.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 比较器 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释比较器：判断两个输入大小并输出逻辑电平；不能简单把普通运放当高速比较器。。
原句Each comparator has a reference voltage which is 1 LSB higher than that of the one below it in the chain.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 比较器 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释比较器：判断两个输入大小并输出逻辑电平；不能简单把普通运放当高速比较器。。
原句For a given input voltage, all the comparators below a certain point will have their input voltage larger than their reference voltage and a “1” lo gic output, and all the compar ators above that point will have a reference voltage larger than the input voltage and a “0 ” logic output.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 比较器 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释比较器：判断两个输入大小并输出逻辑电平；不能简单把普通运放当高速比较器。。
原句The 2 N–1 comparator outputs therefore be have in a way analogous to a mercury thermometer, and the output code at this point is sometimes called a thermometer code.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 比较器 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释比较器：判断两个输入大小并输出逻辑电平；不能简单把普通运放当高速比较器。。
原句Since 2 N–1 data outputs are not really practical, they are pr ocessed by a decoder to generate an N-bit binary output.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Figure 6.50: 3-Bit All-Parallel (Flash) Converter The input signal is applied to all the comparat ors at once, so the th ermometer output is delayed by only one comparator delay from the input, and the encoder N-bit output by only a few gate delays on top of that, so the process is very fast.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 比较器 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释比较器：判断两个输入大小并输出逻辑电平；不能简单把普通运放当高速比较器。。
原句However, the architecture uses large numbers of resist ors and comparators and is limited to low resolutions, and if it is to be fast, each co mparator must run at relatively high power levels.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 比较器。
补充注释比较器：判断两个输入大小并输出逻辑电平；不能简单把普通运放当高速比较器。。
原句Hence, the problems of flash ADCs include limited resolution, high power dissipation because of the large number of high speed comparators (especially at sampling rates greater than 50 MSPS), and relatively large (and therefore expensive) chip sizes.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 比较器 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释比较器：判断两个输入大小并输出逻辑电平；不能简单把普通运放当高速比较器。。
原句In addition, the resistance of the reference resistor chain must be kept low to supply N R PRIORITY ENCODER AND LATCH ANALOG INPUT DIGITAL OUTPUT +VREF R R R R R 0.5R STROBE 1.5R
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 电阻。
补充注释电阻：耗散能量并限制电流的元件参数。。

PDF 第 55 页

展开本页文本译读与注释
原句CONVERTERS ANALOG-TO-DIGITAL CONVERTER ARCHITECTURES 6.51 adequate bias current to the fast comparators, so the voltage reference has to source quite large currents (typically > 10 mA).
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 电压基准、比较器 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释电压基准：提供稳定、低噪声、低漂移的参考电压，是 ADC/DAC 精度链路的一部分。；比较器：判断两个输入大小并输出逻辑电平；不能简单把普通运放当高速比较器。。
原句Each comparator has a voltage -variable junction capacitance, and this signal-dependent capacitance results in most flash ADCs havi ng reduced ENOB and higher distortion at high input frequencies.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 比较器、电容 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释比较器：判断两个输入大小并输出逻辑电平；不能简单把普通运放当高速比较器。；电容：储存电荷的能力；阻抗随频率升高而降低。。
原句For this reason, most flash converters must be driven with a wideband op amp which is tolerant to the cap acitive load presented by the converter as well as high speed transients developed on the input.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 运放。
补充注释运放：高增益差分放大器；分析时先确认是否处于负反馈线性区。。
原句Power dissipation is always a big considera tion in flash converters, especially at resolutions above eight bits.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句A clever t echnique was used AD9410 10-bit, 210 MSPS ADC called interpolation to minimize the number of preamp lifiers in the flash converter comparators and also reduce the power (2.1 W).

原句BASIC LINEAR DESIGN 6.52 Subranging, Error Corrected, and Pipelined ADCs A basic, two-stage N-bit subranging ADC is shown in Figure 6.52.

原句The ADC is based on two separate conversions—a coarse conversion (N1 bits) in the MSB sub-ADC (SADC) followed by a fine conversion (N2 bits) in the LSB sub-ADC.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句Early subranging ADCs nearly always used flash converters as building blocks, but a number of recent ADCs utilize other architectures for the individual ADCs.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 上述概念，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句The conversion process begins placing the sample-and-hold in the hold mode followed by a coarse N1-bit sub-ADC (SADC) conversion of the MSBs.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句The digital outputs of the MSB converter drive an N1-bit sub-DAC (SDAC ) which generates a coarsely quantized version of the analog input signal.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句The N1-bit SDAC output is subtracted from the held analog signal, amplified, and applied to th e N2-bit LSB SADC.

原句The amplifier provides gain, G, sufficient to make the “residue” signal exactly fill the input range of the N2 SADC.

原句The output data from the N1 SADC and the N2 SADC are latched into the output registers yielding the N-bit digital output code, where N = N1 + N2.

原句Figure 6.52: N-bit Two-Stage Subranging ADC In order for this simple subranging architecture to work satisfactorily, both the N1 SADC and SDAC (although they only have N1 bits of resolution) must be better than N-bits accurate.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 安全工作范围、精度或稳定性。

原句The residue signal offset and gain must be adjusted such that it precisely fills the range of the N2 SADC as shown in Figur e 3.66A.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 安全工作范围、精度或稳定性。

原句If the residue signal drifts by more than 1 LSB (referenced to the N2 SADC), th en there will be missi ng codes as shown in Figure 3.66B where the residue signal enters the out-of-range regions labeled “X” and “Y.” Any nonlinearity or drift in the N1 SADC will also cause missing codes if it exceeds 1 LSB referenced to N-bits.

原句In practice, an 8-bit subranging ADC w ith N1 = 4 bits and N2 = 4 bits represents a realistic limit to th is architecture in order to maintain no missing codes over a reasonable operating temperature range.

原句Figure 6.55: Generalized Pipeline Stages in a Subranging ADC with Error Correction The term “pipelined” architectur e refers to the ability of one stage to process data from the previous stage during any given clock cycle.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句At the end of each pha se of a particular clock cycle, the output of a given stage is passed on to the next stage using the T/H functions and new data is shifted into the stage.

原句Of course this means that the digital outputs of all but the last stage in the “pipeline” must be stored in the appropriate number of shift registers so that the digital data arriving at the co rrection logic corresponds to the same sample.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 安全工作范围、精度或稳定性。

原句Notice that the phases of the clocks to the T/H amplifiers are alternated from stage to stage such that when a particular T/H in the ADC enters th e hold mode it holds the sample from the preceding T/H, and the preceding T/H returns to the track mode.

原句If +IN is greater than –IN, cascode-connected transistors Q3, Q6 are on, and Q4, Q6 are off.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 共射共基/共源共栅 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。

原句The differential signal currents therefore flow through the collectors of Q3, Q6 into level-shifting transistors Q7, Q8, and into the output load resistors, deve loping the differential output voltage between +OUT and –OUT.

原句The ramp voltage is compared wi th the input, and when they are equal, a pulse is generated which resets the R/S flip-f lop.

原句At low frequencies, the limits on the performan ce of this VFC are set by the stability of the current source and the monostable timing (which depends on the monostable capacitor, among other things).
中文译读可译为“结果取决于……”。关键依赖项是 稳定性，不能只看单一典型值。
补充注释稳定性：反馈电路不会自激振荡并具有足够瞬态裕量。。
原句The absolute value and temperature stability of the integration capacitor do not a ffect the accuracy, although its leakage and dielectric absorption (DA) do.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 稳定性，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。
补充注释稳定性：反馈电路不会自激振荡并具有足够瞬态裕量。。
原句At high frequencies, second-order effects, such as switching transients in the integrator and the precision of the monostable when it is retriggered very soon after the end of a pulse, take their toll on accuracy and linearity.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句The changeover switch in the current source ad dresses the integrator transient problem.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句By using a changeover switch instead of the on/off switch more common on older VFC designs: (a) there are no on/off transients in the precision current source and (b) the output stage of the integrator sees a constant load—most of the time the current from the source flows directly in the output stage; during charge balance, it still flows in the output stage, but through the integration capacitor.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 上述概念，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句The stability and transient be havior of the precision monosta ble present more problems, but the issue may be avoide d by replacing the monostable with a clocked bistable multivibrator.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 稳定性，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。
补充注释稳定性：反馈电路不会自激振荡并具有足够瞬态裕量。。
原句This arrangement is known as a synchronous VFC or SVFC and is shown in Figure 6.77.
中文译读可译为“这种对象/现象称为……”。本句是在给 一个术语 下定义；后文遇到该词时要回到这个定义。

原句The display of an SVFC output on an oscilloscope is es pecially misleading and is a common cause of confusion—a change of input to a VFC produces a smooth change in the output frequency, but a change to an SVFC produces a change in probability density of output pulses N and N+1 clock cycles after the previous output pulse, which is often misinterpreted as severe jitter and a sign of a faulty device (see Figure 6.78).
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 上述概念，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。

原句Another problem with SVFCs is nonlinearity at output freq uencies related to the clock frequency.

原句Th is problem is intrinsic to SVFCs, but is not often serious : if the circuit card is well laid out, and clock amplitude and dv/dts kept as low as pr actical, the effect is a disc ontinuity in the transfer characteristic of less than 8 LSBs (at 18-bit resolution) at F C/3 and FC/4, and less at other sub-harmonics.

中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句Dual-slope integration has many advantages.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Conversion accuracy is independent of both the capacitance and the clock frequency, becau se they affect both the up-slope and the down-slope by the same ratio.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 电容 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释电容：储存电荷的能力；阻抗随频率升高而降低。。
原句The fixed input signal integra tion period results in rejection of noise frequencies on the analog input that have periods that are equal to or a sub-multiple of the integration time T.
中文译读可译为“二者相等/代数关系成立”。这里的核心量是 噪声，使用时要先统一参考方向和符号。
补充注释噪声：电路内部随机扰动；低噪声设计要分清电压噪声、电流噪声和电阻热噪声。。
原句Proper choice of T can therefore result in excellent rejection of 50-Hz or 60-Hz line ripple as shown in Figure 6.82.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Errors caused by bias currents and the offset voltages of the integrating amplifier and the comparator as well as gain errors can be cancelled by using additi onal charge/discharge cycles to measure “zero” and “full-scale” a nd using the results to digitally correct the initial measurement, as in the quad-slope architecture.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 比较器 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释比较器：判断两个输入大小并输出逻辑电平；不能简单把普通运放当高速比较器。。
原句Figure 6.82: Frequency Response of Integrating ADC
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 频率响应 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释频率响应：电路增益和相位随频率变化的关系。。

原句Because the tracking converter doubly integrates its error signal, the device offers a high degree of noise immunity (12 dB per octave rolloff).

原句The net area under any given noise spike produces an error.

原句However, typical i nductively coupled noise spikes have equal positive and negative going waveforms.

原句CONVERTERS SIGMA-DELTA CONVERTERS 6.85 SECTION 6.3: SIGMA-DELTA CONVERTERS Historical Perspective The sigma-delta (Σ-Δ) ADC architecture had its origins in the early development phases of pulse code modulation (PCM) systems—sp ecifically, those relate d to transmission techniques called delta modulation and differential PCM .
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句(An excellent discussion of both the history and concepts of the sigma- delta ADC can be found by Max Hauser in Reference 1).
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句The driving force behind delta modulation a nd differential PCM was to achieve higher transmission efficiency by transmitting the changes (delta) in value between consecutive samples rather than the actual samples themselves.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句In delta modulation, the analog signal is quantized by a one-bit ADC (a comparator) as shown in Figure 6.85A.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 比较器 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释比较器：判断两个输入大小并输出逻辑电平；不能简单把普通运放当高速比较器。。
原句The comparator output is converted back to an analog signal with a 1-bit DAC, and subtracted from the input after passing through an integrator.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 比较器 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释比较器：判断两个输入大小并输出逻辑电平；不能简单把普通运放当高速比较器。。
原句The shape of the analog signal is transmitted as follows : a 1 indicates that a positive excursion has occurred since the last sample, and a 0 indi cates that a negative excursion has occurred since the last sample.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Figure 6.85: Delta Modulation and Differential PCM If the analog signal remains at a fixed dc le vel for a period of time, a pattern alternating of 0s and 1s is obtained.

原句BASIC LINEAR DESIGN 6.86 exactly the same concept except a multibit ADC is used rather than a comparator to derived the transmitted information.

原句Since there is no limit to the number of pulses of the same sign that may occur, delta modulation systems are capable of tracking sign als of any amplitude.

原句In theory, there is no peak clipping.

原句However, the theoretical limitation of delta modulation is that the analog signal must not change too rapidly.

原句Here, although each sampling inst ant indicates a positive excursion, the analog signal is rising too quickly, and the quantizer is unable to keep pace.

原句SLOPE OVERLOAD Figure 6.86: Quantization Using Delta Modulation Slope clipping can be reduced by increasing the quantum step si ze or increasing the sampling rate.

原句Differential PCM uses a multibit quantizer to effectively increase the quantum step sizes at the increase of complexity.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句Tests have shown that in order to obtain the same quality as classical PCM, delta modul ation requires very high sampling rates, typically 20 × the highest frequency of interest, as opposed to Nyquist rate of 2×.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句For these reasons, delta m odulation and differential PC M have never achieved any significant degree of popularity, however a sl ight modification of the delta modulator leads to the basic sigma-delta architecture, one of the mo st popular high resolution ADC architectures in use today.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 上述概念，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。

原句The basic single and multibit first-order sigma-delta ADC architecture is shown in Figures 6.87A and 6.87B, respectively.

原句Note that the integrator operates on the error signal, whereas in a delta modulator, the inte grator is in the feedback loop.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句The basic oversampling sigma-delta modulator increases the overall signal-to- noise ratio at low frequencies by shaping the quantization noise such that most of it occurs outside the bandwidth of interest.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 带宽、噪声 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释带宽：满足指定增益/误差条件的频率范围。；噪声：电路内部随机扰动；低噪声设计要分清电压噪声、电流噪声和电阻热噪声。。
原句The digital filter then removes the noise outside the bandwidth of interest, and the decimator reduces the output data rate back to the Nyquist rate.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 带宽、噪声 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释带宽：满足指定增益/误差条件的频率范围。；噪声：电路内部随机扰动；低噪声设计要分清电压噪声、电流噪声和电阻热噪声。。

原句Editor's Notes from Analog Dialogue Vol.

原句24-2, 1990, by Dan Sheingold This is not the most earth-shaking of contr oversies, and many readers may wonder what the fuss is all about—if they wonder at all.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句The issue is importa nt to both editor and readers because of the need for consistency; we’d like to use the same name for the same thing whenever it appears.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句But which name? In the case of the modulation technique that led to a new oversampling A/D conversion mechanism, we chose sigma-delta.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 上述概念，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Here’s why. Ordinarily, when a new concept is named by its creators, the name sticks; it should not be changed unless it is erroneous or flies in the face of preceden t.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句The seminal paper on this subject was published in 1962 (Ref erences 9, 10), and its aut hors chose the name “deltasigma modulation,” since it was based on delta modulation but incl uded an integration (summation, hence Σ).
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 上述概念，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Delta-sigma was apparently unchallenged until the 1970s, when engineers at AT&T were publishing papers using the term sigma-delta.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Why? According to Hauser (Reference 1), the precedent had been to name variants of delta modulation with adjectives preceding the word “delta.” Since the form of modul ation in question is a variant of delta modulation, the sigma, used as an adject ive—so the argument we nt—should precede the delta.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Many engineers who came upon the scene subse quently used whatever term caught their fancy, often without knowing why.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句It was even possible to find both terms used interchangeably in the same paper.

原句As matters stand today, sigma-delta is in widespread use, probably for the majority of citations.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句Would its adoption be an injustice to the inventors of the technique?
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句We think not. Like others, we believe that the name delta-sigma is a departure from precedent.

原句Not just in the sense of grammar, but also in relation to the hierarchy of operations.

原句Consider a bloc k diagram for embodying an analog root-mean-square (finding the square root of the mean of a squared signal) computer.

原句First the signal is squared, then it is integrated, and finally it is rooted (see Figure 6.88).
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句If we were to name the overall function after the causal order of operations, it would have to be called a “square mean root” function.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 安全工作范围、精度或稳定性。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句But naming in order of the hierarchy of its mathematical operations gives us the familiar—and undisputed—name, root meansquare.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 上述概念，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。

原句Consider now a block diagram for ta king a difference (delta), and then integrating it (sigma).
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句Its causal order would give delta-sigma, but in functional hierarchy it is sigma-delta, since it computes the integral of a difference.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 上述概念，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。

原句We believe that the latter term is correct and follows precedent; and we have adopted it as our standard.

原句CONVERTERS SIGMA-DELTA CONVERTERS 6.89 Figure 6.88: Sigma-Delta (Σ-Δ) or Delta-Sigma (Δ-Σ)?

原句BASIC LINEAR DESIGN 6.90 Basics of Sigma-Delta ADCs Sigma-Delta Analog-Digital Converters ( Σ-Δ ADCs) have been known for over thirty years, but only recently has the technology (high density digital VLSI) existed to manufacture them as inexpensive monolithic in tegrated circuits.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 上述概念，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句They are now used in many applications where a low cost, low bandwidth, low power, high resolution ADC is required.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 带宽。
补充注释带宽：满足指定增益/误差条件的频率范围。。
原句There have been innumerable descripti ons of the architect ure and theory of Σ-Δ ADCs, but most commence with a maze of integrals an d deteriorate from there.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 上述概念，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Some engineers who do not understand the theory of operation of Σ-Δ ADCs and are convinced, from study of a typical published article, which it is too complex to comprehend easily.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句There is nothing particularly difficult to understand about Σ-Δ ADCs, as long as you avoid the detailed mathematics, and this section has been written in an attempt to clarify the subject.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句A Σ-Δ ADC contains very simple analog electronics (a comparator, voltage reference, a switch, and one or more integrat ors, and analog summing circuits), and quite complex digital computational circuitry.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 电压基准、比较器 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释电压基准：提供稳定、低噪声、低漂移的参考电压，是 ADC/DAC 精度链路的一部分。；比较器：判断两个输入大小并输出逻辑电平；不能简单把普通运放当高速比较器。。
原句Th is circuitry consists of a digital signal processor (DSP) which acts as a filter (general ly, but not invariably, a low-pass filter).
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 上述概念，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句It is not necessary to know precisely how the filter works to appreciate what it does.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句To understand how a Σ-Δ ADC works, familiarity with the concepts of oversampling, quantization noise shaping, digital filtering, and decimation is required.
中文译读可译为“必须/需要……”。这是约束条件，通常关系到 噪声。
补充注释噪声：电路内部随机扰动；低噪声设计要分清电压噪声、电流噪声和电阻热噪声。。
原句Let us consider the technique of over-sampling with an analysis in the frequency domain.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句Where a dc conversion has a quantization error of up to ½ LSB, a sampled data system has quantization noise.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 噪声 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释噪声：电路内部随机扰动；低噪声设计要分清电压噪声、电流噪声和电阻热噪声。。
原句A perfect classical N-bit samp ling ADC has an rms quantization noise of q/√12 uniformly distributed within the Nyquist band of dc to fs/2 (where q is the value of an LSB and f s is the sampling rate) as shown in Figure 6.89A.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 噪声 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释噪声：电路内部随机扰动；低噪声设计要分清电压噪声、电流噪声和电阻热噪声。。
原句Therefore, its SNR with a full-scale sinewave input will be (6.02N + 1.76) dB.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
补充注释学习提示：先确认这句话是在给定义、列条件、还是给结论；电路题里最常见的错误是跳过适用条件直接套公式。
原句If the ADC is less than perfect, and its noise is greater than its th eoretical minimum quantization noise, then its effective resolution will be less than N-bits.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 噪声 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释噪声：电路内部随机扰动；低噪声设计要分清电压噪声、电流噪声和电阻热噪声。。
原句Its actual resolution (often known as its Effective Number of Bits or ENOB) will be defined by: dB02.6 dB76.1SNRENOB −= .
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句Eq. 6.15 If we choose a much higher sampling rate, Kf s (see Figure 6.89B), the rms quantization noise remains q/√12, but the noise is now distributed over a wider bandwidth dc to Kf s/2.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 带宽、噪声，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。
补充注释带宽：满足指定增益/误差条件的频率范围。；噪声：电路内部随机扰动；低噪声设计要分清电压噪声、电流噪声和电阻热噪声。。
原句If we then apply a digital low-pass filter (LPF) to the output, we remove much of the quantization noise, but do not a ffect the wanted signal—so the ENOB is improved.
中文译读可译为“但是/然而……”。本句在提醒限制或例外：即使涉及 噪声，也不能无条件套用前一句结论。
补充注释噪声：电路内部随机扰动；低噪声设计要分清电压噪声、电流噪声和电阻热噪声。。
原句We have accomplished a high resolution A/D c onversion with a low resolution ADC.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句The factor K is generally referred to as the oversampling ratio.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。

原句It should be noted at this point that oversampling has an added benefit in that it relaxes the re quirements on the analog antialiasing filter.

原句CONVERTERS SIGMA-DELTA CONVERTERS 6.91 Figure 6.89: Oversampling, Digital Filtering, Noise Shaping, and Decimation Since the bandwidth is reduced by the digital output filter, the out put data rate may be lower than the original sampling rate (Kf s) and still satisfy the Nyquist criterion.
中文译读逐句译读：这句话围绕 带宽、噪声 展开；核心是在说明这些量/结构之间的定义、作用或约束关系。
补充注释带宽：满足指定增益/误差条件的频率范围。；噪声：电路内部随机扰动；低噪声设计要分清电压噪声、电流噪声和电阻热噪声。。
原句This may be achieved by passing every Mth result to the output and discarding the remainder.
中文译读逐句译读：这是一句上下文说明，建议与前后句连读，用来补全本节的概念背景或推导条件。
